facts.description: Valid customer accounts signed up from 2026-08-01 through 2026-08-07 after exclusions.
facts.canonical_answer: 12 valid customer accounts in the August 1-7 cohort
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if the cohort size is communicated as 12 valid/customer accounts. It may appear as a KPI, denominator, or short label.

facts.description: Share of cohort accounts that completed both activation events within seven days of signup.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if activation is shown as 75%, 75.0%, or 9/12. It may appear through KPI cards, chart labels, or text; exact wording is not required.

facts.description: Share of cohort accounts with an active event during days 22-28 after signup.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if week-4/W4 retention is shown as about 67%, 66.7%, or 8/12. Must be retention, not activation or all-month activity.

facts.description: Plan segment with the highest week-4 retention rate.
facts.canonical_answer: Enterprise has the strongest week-4 retention, 4 of 4 / 100%
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if Enterprise is identified as the plan segment with strongest/highest week-4 retention. Values may be shown as 100%, 4/4, or natural language such as perfect retention.

facts.description: Retained accounts that used automation_run during their week-4 window.
facts.canonical_answer: 7 of 8 retained accounts used automation in week 4, 87.5%
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if automation adoption among retained accounts is shown as about 88%, 87.5%, or 7/8. The denominator must be retained accounts, not the whole cohort, unless the wording still makes the retained-account basis clear.

facts.description: Concise insight based on computed onboarding and retention results.
expected_value: Enterprise retention is perfect, and automation is common among retained accounts.
Activation is 75%, but week-4 retention is strongest in Enterprise.
Automation usage is a strong retained-account signal, especially in Enterprise.
facts.canonical_answer: Takeaway should connect Enterprise retention and/or automation adoption to product focus
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept any concise product takeaway grounded in the activation, week-4 retention, plan-segment, and automation findings.

facts.description: Do not use employee, demo, cancelled, forecast, old-cohort, or outside-window events as final answers.
facts.canonical_answer: Do not use invalid SaaS cohort accounts or outside-window events as final results
facts.semantic_acceptance: Strictly reject or cap if employee/demo/cancelled/forecast/old-cohort accounts, all accounts, or outside-window events are treated as final cohort, activation, retention, or adoption results. Mentions that such records were excluded are allowed.

calculation_notes: Build the cohort from accounts with signup_date 2026-08-01 through 2026-08-07, account_type=customer, status in active/trial, and source_record=actual. Exclude employee, demo, cancelled, forecast, and old-cohort accounts. Activation requires both workspace_created and first_report_published within seven days of signup. Activated accounts are A01, A03, A04, A05, A07, A09, A10, A11, A12, so activation=9/12=75.0%. Week-4 retention requires at least one active event (dashboard_view, report_export, automation_run, or integration_sync) from day 22 through day 28 after signup. Retained accounts are A01, A03, A05, A06, A07, A09, A11, A12, so week-4 retention=8/12=66.7%. Plan retention rates: Pro=2/4=50.0%, Team=2/4=50.0%, Enterprise=4/4=100.0%; Enterprise is strongest. Automation adoption among retained accounts is A01, A03, A05, A07, A09, A11, A12 = 7/8 = 87.5%.
